heaviest_rainfall.status: Record-breaking rain fell on 09 November 2025 with a total accumulation of 47.89mm over 144.8 hours and falling at a rate of 24.6mm per hour at times.

heaviest_rainrate.status: 34.3mm per hour is the maximum rain rate ever recorded on 02 September 2025, during a 15.0 hour storm which dumped 21.19mm of rain from the clouds.

longest_rain.status: Rain fell for 144.8 hours on 09 November 2025 creating a new record that still lasts.  The storm produced a total of 47.89mm with a maximum intensity of 24.6mm per hour.

most_lightning.status: Since records began on 11 April 2025, the highest lightning strike count in one day is 225 on 28 May 2026.

storm.status: No storm activity at the moment.  The previous storm occurred on 18 July 2026 and lasted 1 day.

top_10_dry.status: We are in the midst of one of the longest dry periods recorded.  This dry period is ranked 2nd since records began in 23 September 2024.  The current stretch started on 03 July 2026 and has lasted 19 days.  The longest dry period started on 24 April 2025 and lasted for 19 days.

top_10_rainy.status: The longest rainy period started on 13 January 2026 and lasted for 22 days.

cold_snaps.status: There is not a cold snap right now.  There have been 3 this year, compared to a usual average of 3.7.

extreme.status: The highest recorded temperature difference is 27.7°C from 24 April 2026 (when the temperature reached 23.2°C but fell to -4.5°C).

heatwaves.status: There is not an active heatwave.  There has been 3 so far this year, while the average is 1.9.

tropical.status: The last tropical night was recorded on 28 June 2026.  The total number of occurrences since 1973 number is only 68.

average.status: The current average daily wind direction is south-westerly (210°).  In the UK, a south-westerly wind often means mild and wet weather.

gusts.status: The top recorded wind gust is 128.2 mph on 27 January 2026.

run.status: 1737 miles is the largest wind run recorded on 21 October 2012.

speed.status: The largest wind speed ever was on 13 October 2012 with a value of 93.7 mph.

top_10_windy.status: The longest windy period started on 11 October 2012 and lasted for 21 days.

summary.description:
Today, cooler for the feels like temperature (down 9.2°C) and outside temperature (down 7.8°C) and more moisture-laden outside humidity (up 21%).

Compared to today, yesterday also had noticeable differences in light levels (up 28381 lux) and cloud levels (up 2673 m).

The past week and month had observable variation in light levels (up 25940 lux), cloud levels (up 1876 m), and outside humidity (down 8%) when compared to today.

cloud levels.description:
Today’s average cloud levels is 2670m lower than yesterday, 1876.3m lower than the past week, 1187.1m lower than the past month.

The cloud levels yesterday fell between 1974m minimum and 8460m maximum, with an average of 2510m; weekly and monthly averages were marginally higher at 4390m and 3700m.
cloud levels.summary: A high cloud base will mean mostly sunny conditions.

summary.summary: Inside; comfortable and pleasant temperatures. Outside; you can expect comfortable, mild temperatures. Also, the air will feel dry and little or no wind. Showers, a high cloud base will mean mostly sunny conditions, and overcast conditions will limit sunlight. Similar weather conditions should remain.

washing.phrase: The conditions are favourable for drying your laundry outside right now but, as always, monitor the situation for changes to the weather.
